title: Scrapy Architecture, Scheduler, Middleware, AutoThrottle, and Observability · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Trace engine, scheduler, downloader, spider, item pipeline, signals, extensions, requests, responses, failures, and ownership in Scrapy.
Map canonical request identity, priorities, persistence, depth, retries, duplicate filtering, and resume semantics into scheduler evidence.
Reason about request and response traversal order, built-in middleware, exceptions, short-circuiting, and settings merge before customization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

debug case — A debug site-quality team handles case 1854 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. options.text: A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Crawl Observability.

Another successful Crawl Observability run of the same Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan happy path. That claim fails the crawl observability · fault isolation evidence boundary for Crawl Observability.

For crawl observability · fault isolation, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Crawl Observability for Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crawl Observability
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-crawl-observability-14pbf0" that uses case 5576 to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crawl Observability.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Crawl Observability into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crawl Observability, keep Crawl Observability connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-crawl-observability-14pbf0
# Competency: scraper-crawl-observability.
# Case 5576: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def evidence_scraper_crawl_observability_14pbf0(component_boundaries: int = 8, verified_boundaries: int = 8, middleware_orders: int = 6, metric_families: int = 10, hidden_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_14pbf0 = "14pbf0-0fbp41-5576"
    assert evidence_variant_14pbf0
    evidence_axes_14pbf0 = (
        "authorization14pbf0",
        "url5576",
        "response0fbp41",
        "tree14pbf05576",
        "frontier557614pbf0",
        "report0fbp415576",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_14pbf0)) == 6
    if component_boundaries < 6 or verified_boundaries < 0:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "scrapy-architecture"
    if verified_boundaries != component_boundaries:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "component-reconciliation"
    if middleware_orders < 4 or metric_families < 8:
        return False, metric_families, "scale-evidence"
    if hidden_failures > 0:
        return False, hidden_failures, "observability-gap"
    assert verified_boundaries == component_boundaries and hidden_failures == 0
    return True, metric_families, "observable-scrapy-transfer"
